```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Truck Driver Wage Search Surge",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'truck driver jobs' and 'truck driver salary'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in truck driver job and wage searches signal labor market tightening in freight, often preceding freight rate increases and logistics sector rallies.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Tight trucking labor markets correlate with stronger freight demand and pricing power for logistics and industrial companies."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Google Trends index for 'truck driver jobs' or 'truck driver salary' exceeds its 12-week median by 40% or more",
      "exit": "Close after 3 weeks or when index falls below 20% above median",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Labor shortages in transportation are chronic; wage/job search spikes occur 4–6 times per year as seasonal demand fluctuates."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Manufacturing Input Cost Pressure FRED Acceleration",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series PPI for intermediate materials (PPIICMM) weekly aggregation",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Week-over-week acceleration in PPI for inputs signals rising cost pressures, which trigger equity rotation out of materials and into defensive sectors.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Materials companies face margin compression when input costs accelerate faster than they can pass through prices."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 2-week change in PPI intermediate materials exceeds the 26-week rolling average change by 0.3% or more",
      "exit": "Exit after 2 weeks or when acceleration reverses below median",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "FRED PPI data releases weekly; commodity and energy volatility produce input cost swings frequently enough to trigger monthly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Buildout Acceleration Play",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region weekly snapshot",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid week-over-week growth in public EV charger deployment signals infrastructure capex cycles and local clean energy investment momentum.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "EV charging buildout drives industrial capex, construction jobs, and equipment orders for utilities and industrial suppliers."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly EV charger additions exceed 52-week median by 50% or more in any 3-state cluster",
      "exit": "After 4 weeks or if weekly additions fall back to median",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Regional infrastructure grants and utility capex cycles create recurring deployment bursts; statistically fires 2–3 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Load Factor Collapse Fear Trade",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS airline load factor and capacity data weekly aggregation",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp 2+ week drop in airline load factors signals demand weakness and signals bearish travel/discretionary sentiment.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Airline load factor collapse is a leading indicator of consumer travel pullback and discretionary spending weakness."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly airline load factor drops 2+ points below its 8-week moving average",
      "exit": "Exit after 3 weeks or when load factor rebounds above 8-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal travel demand swings and economic uncertainty produce 3–4 load factor dips per year of 2+ points."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Unemployment Claims Spike Jobless Fear Burst",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED initial jobless claims (ICSA) daily/weekly release",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Week-over-week jump in jobless claims exceeding seasonal adjustment by 15% or more signals labor market stress and consumer anxiety.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Jobless claims spikes correlate with consumer pullback on discretionary spending and equity de-risking."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly jobless claims spike >15% above prior week and exceed 52-week median by 20%",
      "exit": "Exit after 2 weeks or when claims revert to median",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Jobless claims volatility is weekly and frequent; threshold fires 6–10 times per year during normal volatility."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Commodity Shortage News Volume Burst",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS/news feed count for 'supply chain bottleneck' and 'commodity shortage' articles via rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Daily spike in supply chain and commodity shortage news mentions signals market anxiety about logistics constraints and inflation.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Shortage narratives boost commodity and materials equity valuations as investors price in scarcity premiums."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily news article count for 'supply chain' OR 'commodity shortage' exceeds 90-day median by 60% or more",
      "exit": "Exit after 5 trading days or when article count falls to median",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Geopolitical events, weather, and logistics disruptions reliably produce supply chain news spikes weekly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Stock Buyback Momentum Reversion Signal",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK, XLY, XLC relative to SPY",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Multi-day surge in relative trading volume on mega-cap tech/growth stocks often coincides with corporate buyback windows; pullback signals mean reversion.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Buyback-driven rallies are mechanical; when volume normalizes, momentum fades and reverses."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If XLK or XLC volume exceeds 30-day median by 40% for 2 consecutive days AND price up >1%, short on day 3",
      "exit": "Exit after 5 trading days or if price rallies another 2%",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Corporate buyback windows occur quarterly; volume spikes happen 2–3 times per month during window periods."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Weather Extreme Disruption Energy Demand Spike",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly by region + EIA electricity demand",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Days with extreme heat/cold >2 std dev from normal drive electricity demand spikes and boost utility/energy pricing.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Extreme weather drives peak electricity demand and margin expansion for utilities and power generators."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily temperature anomaly >2 std dev from 20-year normal for region AND EIA electricity demand spike >8% from prior day",
      "exit": "Exit after 3 trading days or when temperature normalizes",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal temperature extremes produce 3–6 multi-day heat/cold waves annually; each fires signal reliably."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Port Container Backlog Volatility Freight Rally",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Port of LA/Long Beach container volume weekly data via port_container_volume adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Week-over-week swings in port container throughput >15% signal volatility in global trade flows and logistics demand.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Port volatility creates urgency for shippers to use alternative routes/carriers; freight rates rise."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly port container volume swings >15% above or below 13-week moving average",
      "exit": "Exit after 4 weeks or when volatility normalizes to <8% of MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Container volume swings are seasonal and event-driven; fires 2–4 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Swarm Construction Material Demand Surge",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity API for daily counts and magnitude by region",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Multi-day earthquake swarms (>5 events in 48 hrs, magnitude 3.5+) in urban areas signal impending rebuilding demand and construction/materials activity.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Post-earthquake reconstruction drives cement, steel, and materials demand."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily earthquake count in a metro region >5 in 48 hours with avg magnitude >3.5",
      "exit": "Exit 10 trading days later or if swarm subsides for 5+ days",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seismic swarms occur 2–4 times per month globally; US-based swarms fire 1–2 times per month."
  }
]
```
